Why My Blog Wouldn't Update: A Service Workers Stale-Cache Story
I noticed my blog kept "not updating" for some readers, and it turned out the culprit was Service Workers. Writing this up so the next person hits less of a wall.
A bunch of friends and readers were asking me things like:
"Ray, you haven't updated your blog in forever, right? It looks frozen."
Which is obviously not true — I post regularly, and I'd just wrapped up an iThome ironman series. So I asked them for screenshots, and their views all looked like this:
First guess — browser cache. I walked them through clearing Chrome's cache.
Then I dropped it. Until a few days later, a student told me:
"Hey, your blog content keeps flipping back and forth."
Now I was interested. I asked for a screen recording.
You can see the student did do a hard refresh + cache clear — but the moment they hit F5 again, it reverted to the old version.
First suspect was Cloudflare, since my domain sits behind it and Cloudflare has its own cache. But I could reproduce the same behavior on
doesn't go through Cloudflare. So that was out.

Then I noticed — Firefox was fine. Only Chromium-based browsers had the issue. So Chrome had to be doing something special, and the first thing that came to mind was PWA. Back when I cared more about UX, I'd set up PWA support on my blog. Later I dropped it because it was slowing down content updates — but apparently I didn't clean up after myself.
The fix is straightforward — open DevTools, go to the
know to unregister? Most won't open DevTools. So I added a small script to my
Now whenever a visitor lands on my blog, any leftover Service Worker gets auto-unregistered. Problem solved on their end too.

Quick explainer on why Service Workers caused this. Service Workers are the core of Progressive Web Apps (PWA) — they let a website behave like an app, including working offline, by caching content aggressively.

So when I dropped PWA support but never unregistered the Service Worker for existing users, their browsers kept happily serving cached content from the dead SW and never bothered fetching fresh data from my site. That's why the blog "stopped updating" for them.

Readers kept telling me my blog hadn't been updated in ages — but I was posting regularly. After some digging it turned out to be a Service Workers issue left over from when I'd dropped PWA from my blog. This post walks through how I diagnosed it, how to manually unregister Service Workers in Chrome DevTools, and how to auto-unregister them for visitors so they don't get stuck on a stale cache.
